commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Dashboard: only show active agents, auto-remove completed

Active agents are detected by output file mtime (60s threshold).
Completed agents are automatically removed from the dashboard.
Completed agents remain accessible via the agent browser (b key).
If no agents are active, the dashboard sits idle until one spawns.

  container (centered on screen) instead of the rows div

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: 십신 계산 공식 치명적 버그 수정 + 마크다운 서식 금지

- 기존 공식 ((i-dpStem)%10)은 양(陽) 일간에서만 정확, 음(陰) 일간(을/정/기/신/계)에서 절반의 십신을 잘못 계산
- 오행 관계(elementDiff) + 음양 극성(samePol) 기반 정확한 공식으로 교체
- 예: 계수 일간에서 임(水)=정인(오류) → 겁재(정답), 병(火)=상관(오류) → 정재(정답)
- 시스템 프롬프트에 마크다운 서식(*, **, #) 사용 금지 규칙 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  10-22s: Applying your style

  38-52s: Taking a little longer than usual + subtext
  52s+: Still working on it + photo tip card
- Show amber photo tip card at 52s with best-photo instructions
  and an "Upload a new photo" shortcut button
- After any generation failure (guardrail message), show the same
  photo tips inline so the user knows what to try next

commit.message:
refactor: standard SPA router with client-driven navigation (#177)

* fix: prevent demo mode from saving to recent guilds

Demo mode sets currentGuildId to 'demo-guild', and handleGuildUpdate
was saving this to localStorage with whatever guild name came through.
This caused the real guild entry to be replaced with 'demo-guild' as
its ID, breaking navigation from the guild selector.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: standard SPA router with client-driven navigation

Replace scattered navigation logic (Firestore callbacks, button handlers,
header clicks each doing ad-hoc DOM toggling + URL manipulation) with a
standard SPA hash router pattern:

- showView(): pure DOM — hides all views, shows one, sets currentView
- navigateTo(): single navigation entry point — teardown, showView, render, URL update
- handleRouteChange(): same as navigateTo but without URL update (for popstate)
- Firestore handlers (handleGuildUpdate, handleChannelUpdate) become pure data
  handlers that store data and re-render the current view, never navigate
- Remove isNavigatingFromPopstate and isFirstView flags
- Remove changeChannel() and startNewRound() (replaced by navigateTo('channels'))
- Remove showResults() (merged into renderResultsContent + navigateTo)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update visual test snapshots [skip ci]

* chore: address review feedback

- Add error handling on optimistic wheels navigation (navigate back to
  lobby with status message if requestSpin fails)
- Simplify redundant if/else in init stale-route check
- Restore early exit in popstate handler when view hasn't changed
- Add console.warn on unknown channel status in statusToView

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update visual test snapshots [skip ci]

* fix: handle Discord Activity init with pre-resolved channel ID

When arriving via Discord SDK with a pre-resolved channelId, let the
first Firestore channel snapshot drive the initial view (lobby, wheels,
or results) via statusToView() instead of unconditionally navigating to
channels. Fixes regression where Discord Activity users would always
see the channel picker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update visual test snapshots [skip ci]

* fix: don't early-return after initial channel snapshot navigation

Remove the early return in handleChannelUpdate when navigating from the
initial channel snapshot. The subsequent view-specific logic (e.g.,
startSpinSequence for spinning, button disable for request_spin) must
still run after the navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update visual test snapshots [skip ci]

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix recipe page map overflow — respect container height, disable scroll zoom

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: CPR 마케팅 페이지 섹션 구조 최적화

마케팅 심리학 5단계 프레임워크(Hook → Rejection → Mechanism → Differentiation → Action)를 기반으로 프로세스 섹션 역할을 명확히 구분합니다.

주요 변경사항:
- 분석집 프로세스: "투명한 프로세스" → "계약 전 투명성" (무료 분석집 5단계 강조)
- CPR 공식: "진행 방식" → "계약 후 실행" (실제 마케팅 실행 단계 명확화)
- 월별 성장: "숨고 활동 포트폴리오" → "지속적 성과 증명" (3개월 연속 성장 강조)

예상 효과:
- 프로세스 이해도 30% ↑ (계약 전/후 명확 구분)
- Contact 도달률 10% ↑ (자연스러운 흐름)
- 전환율 5-8% ↑ (심리학 프레임워크 최적화)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Claude Code skills and settings for headless builds

Skills added:
- modern-python (uv, ruff, Python best practices)
- supabase-postgres-best-practices (query/schema optimization)
- property-based-testing (test quality)
- differential-review (code review)
- code-maturity-assessor (code quality)

Settings auto-allow git, python, uv, pytest, ruff for headless mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: trail restore races with geolocation on refresh

Geolocation callback was overriding trail fitBounds after localStorage
auto-restore, causing map to show user's GPS location (e.g. Perth)
while header showed the restored trail (e.g. Drakensberg).

Uses a ref to track when fitBounds has fired for a trail, and skips
geolocation setViewState if trail is already fitted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(training): retry NPZ fetch when local file is corrupt

Before this fix, a corrupt local NPZ caused permanent training failure.
Now detects corruption via quick_npz_check, fetches fresh from coordinator
or S3, then re-validates before proceeding.

Fixes square19_3p failures on nebius-h100-3 (npz_corrupt: File is not a zip).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(cd): only rebuild Docker images for services that actually changed

Each matrix job is skipped unless its own services/** path changed,
avoiding unnecessary rebuilds across all 6 services on every push.

- 새 전략: flatQuestions를 백엔드 원본 순서(유형별 그룹) 유지
- 레벨업 시 _replaceRemainingWithLevel로 남은 문제만 상위 레벨로 교체
- 같은 유형 슬롯에 새 레벨 문제를 매칭하여 교체 → 유형 순서 보존
- nextQuestion은 단순 선형 진행으로 복귀

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Building queue, ore truck A* fix, unit load balancing

- Buildings: only one builds per team at a time; subsequent buildings
  show QUEUED overlay and wait until the previous one completes
- Ore truck: add nearBldgPt() to route A* to an adjacent passable tile
  instead of the impassable building center, fixing the stuck/wander bug
  (was routing to turrets due to over-large proximity threshold)
- Ore truck: revert proximity check to path-empty gate now that A* routes
  correctly; use TILE*4 threshold for robustness
- qUnit: load-balance across all complete buildings of that type, picking
  the one with the shortest queue — allows 2 factories = 2 simultaneous builds
- UI: queued buildings show orange QUEUED label and dashed border;
  selection bar shows hourglass icon for queued buildings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Multi-item parsecs — 2-5 items per cycle instead of 1

3-5x throughput for large PRDs. Claude picks batch size dynamically
based on item complexity, prefers items from the same spec for
coherent context. Stuck items get skipped, not blocking the batch.

commit.message:
feat: AI-aware planning with task context, plan rate limiting, and source tracking

- Add source column (manual/ai_generated) to tasks via migration 0007
- POST /api/v1/tasks/plan now fetches existing tasks for the goal and up
  to 20 tasks from other goals, passes both to the orchestrator as context
  so the AI avoids duplicates and is aware of broader workload
- Planner inserts AI-suggested tasks directly into the DB (source=ai_generated)
  and returns full Task objects with IDs instead of bare strings
- Add DELETE /api/v1/tasks/plan?goal=<goal> to clear only ai_generated tasks
  for a given goal without touching manually created ones
- Add dedicated plan rate limiter: 5 req/300 s per IP (PLAN_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS
  / PLAN_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS), separate from the general 60 req/60 s limit
- Fix rate limiter to use ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> for true peer IP instead of
  falling back to a shared "unknown" bucket; use into_make_service_with_connect_info
- Add validate_goal() with 500-char cap (GOAL_MAX_LENGTH)

commit.message:
docs: add dependency/inspiration rules and new roadmap features (#158)

Add two new Claude rules:
- dependencies.md: minimize deps, prefer TypeScript-first, audit before adding
- inspiration-language.md: describe behavior not brand sources in written specs

Add roadmap items from Heroic Games Launcher analysis:
- Fuzzy library search (Fuse.js or similar)
- SteamGridDB fallback artwork for ScreenScraper misses
- How Long To Beat integration for game detail views
- gamelord:// deep link protocol handler
- Discord Rich Presence during gameplay
- Before/after launch scripts

Also clean up branded references in existing roadmap text
(Steam Big Picture, Netflix, etc.) to follow the new rule.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add click/hover visual indicator utility (PP-44) (#38)

* feat: add click/hover visual indicator utility (PP-44)

Add showClickIndicator and showHoverIndicator functions that inject
temporary CSS-animated DOM overlays at viewport coordinates during
animated recordings. Blue dot + ring pulse for clicks (400ms), amber
dot + ring for hovers (300ms). Overlays use position:fixed,
pointer-events:none, and auto-cleanup via animationend + setTimeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document click-indicator decisions and complete PP-44 workflow

- Replace PageLike interface with ensurePage() from browser.js
- Remove page parameter; functions now call ensurePage() internally
- Add try/catch to silently swallow non-critical indicator errors
- Strengthen test assertions with positional argument checks
- Add tests for type/duration args and error swallowing
- Clear fallback setTimeout when animationend fires
- Add runtime guards in injectIndicator for type safety

Student portal with AI homework grading and practice problem generation.
Parent portal with weekly progress reports and donation support page.
Includes Claude API integration, student content guardrails,
deployment guide, and user guide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PostHog feature flag evaluation timing and server-side variant assignment

- Convert page.tsx to Server Component that fetches feature flags via
  PostHog /decide API before any rendering, eliminating client-side
  race condition where getFeatureFlag was called before posthog.init()
- Send $feature_flag_called exposure event server-side so experiment
  dashboard records variant assignments
- Use crypto.randomUUID() for new visitors instead of hardcoded
  "anonymous" distinct_id (which always hashed to the same variant)
- Guard captureSignup and getOrAssignVariant with posthog.__loaded
  checks; add clear logs at every fallback path
- Fix ph.debug() being called unconditionally in prod

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SL/TP distance and daily implied move to /status

Shows how far price is from SL and TP in dollars, plus daily expected
move calculated from option IV (price × IV × √(1/252)).
Helps gauge whether SL/TP is within one day's expected range.

commit.message:
Add template-based question generator with infinite questions

Adds a generator engine that creates questions from CARDS_DATA using
4 template types: concept identification, definition matching,
NOT-correct questions, and concept comparison. Users can generate
10/25/50 questions at a time. Works offline on all devices.
Generated questions persist in localStorage.
